summary: Package hijacks the `node` runtime command via bin entry
details: package.json declares `bin: { "node": "./shim.js" }`, registering a binary literally named `node`. On global install — or via any tool that places package bins on PATH ahead of the system runtime — subsequent invocations of `node` execute the package's shim instead of the real Node.js interpreter, handing control of every future `node <script>` call to package-controlled code. The shipped shim (shim.js, shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bun`) branches on process.platform and uses child_process.execSync to open https://github.com/X3r0Day/BunnyHijack in the user's browser and launch the OS calculator (`calc` / `gnome-calculator` / `open -a Calculator`) — a proof-of-concept payload that demonstrates arbitrary command execution under the hijacked `node` name. The hijack of a core runtime command, not the current PoC payload, is the supply-chain harm: any version published under this name can substitute arbitrary code for the system Node.js interpreter on machines where the package's bin directory precedes the real runtime on PATH.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
